title: Finalizers should not be empty
description: Finalizers should not be emptyAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellperformanceWhy is this an issue?Finalizers come with a performance cost due to the overhead of tracking the life cycle of objects. An empty one is consequently costly with no benefit or justification.Noncompliant code examplepublic class Foo { ~Foo() // Noncompliant { } }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects empty finalizers (destructors) in C# classes by checking ClassDeclaration source for destructor patterns with empty bodies.
never_trigger_on: Destructors with at least one statement in the body
logic.reasoning: Detects C# destructors (finalizers) that are declared but have no code in their body. Since the scanner doesn't extract destructors as separate nodes, we check ClassDeclaration source for the pattern ~ClassName() { } (empty body). We exclude destructors with content by checking for non-empty bodies.
